reasoning: Read RBARON.MAC:15-18 character by character: `.MACRO .4WORD .A,.B,...,.K` / `.WORD .A*4,.B*4,.C*4,.D*4,.E*4,.F*4,.G*4` / `.WORD .H*4,.I*4,.J*4,.K*4` / `.ENDM`. It does multiply every operand by 4, and RBARON.MAC:5930 is `POTDLY:	.4WORD -32.,-23.,...` — .4WORD, not .WORD. Radix: line 5930 sits inside the hex region (.RADIX 16 at :74; the only decimal island is :6217-6280, back to hex at :6281), and the trailing-period operands are decimal literals while the bare -5/0/4/8 read identically in either radix, so the operand list is unambiguous. POTSCL indexes it as a WORD table (RBARON.MAC:5865-5871: `TXA / ASL / TAX / LDA AX,POTDLY -> PLDELY / LDA AX,POTDLY+1 -> PLDELY+1`) and PLDELY is `.BLKB 2` (:165) — a 16-bit signed value, exactly the headroom the x4 needs. Real table = [-128,-92,-68,-40,-20,0,16,32,52,72,100]. Unit check passes in our favour: PLDELY is added straight to I4YPOS (RBARON.MAC:3245-3252), and our `altitude` IS I4YPOS (ALT_MIN=0x8*4=32, ALT_MAX=0x180*4=1536, matching PLYMAX=180*4 / PLYMIN=8*4 at RBARON.MAC:446-447 in the hex region), so ours and the ROM's are the same unit and the 4x is a genuine 4x, not a units artefact. flight.ts:43-45 holds the raw operands. Governing code: pitchDelta() is the only producer of pitchRate and is live via step(). Held.
corrections: A naked table swap is NOT the whole fix. PITCH_SCALE (flight.ts:97, PI/512) converts pitchRate to the camera's transient pitch angle and was calibrated against the un-multiplied operands; quadrupling the table without dividing PITCH_SCALE by 4 will quadruple the visual nose tilt, which is a separate (and unsourced) quantity. Divide PITCH_SCALE to PI/2048 in the same commit to hold the rendered pitch constant while the altitude rate becomes correct.

reasoning: Decoded DISCHK (RBARON.MAC:3468-3496) myself. Entry: A=LSB, TEMP1=MSB; `TAX / LDA TEMP1 / STX TEMP2 / BIT TEMP3`. BIT loads N<-bit7 and V<-bit6 of TEMP3. `BVS 10$` (V=D6) and `BMI 10$` (N=D7) both branch away; only D5 (0x20) sets neither and falls through to `TXA / ASL / TAX / LDA TEMP1 / ADC TEMP1` = a 16-bit x2. At 10$: `JSR DIVBY4` (an arithmetic >>2 via `CMP I,80 / ROR / ROR TEMP2`, twice — sign preserving) then `BIT TEMP3 / BVS 20$`: the NON-BVS path (reached only via BMI, i.e. D7) does `SEC / SBC TEMP2 ... SBC TEMP2+1` = v - v/4 = 0.75v; 20$ (D6) does `CLC / ADC TEMP2 ... ADC TEMP2+1` = v + v/4 = 1.25v. Absolute gains: D5=2.0, D6=1.25, D7=0.75. Normalised on the x2 fall-through: D5=1.0, D6=0.625, D7=0.375 — which reproduces the source's own inline comments exactly (`BVS 10$ ;0.625 * VALUE`, `BMI 10$ ;0.375 * VALUE`, `ADC TEMP1 ;1.0 RANGE`). Branch polarity attacked and it holds. The bit->distance mapping independently corroborates: PFMOTN (RBARON.MAC:3179-3189) does `LDA I,20 / CPX I,8*3 / BCS 10$ / ASL / CPX I,4*3 / BCS 10$ / ASL`, so a LARGE distance metric X (>=24) keeps 0x20=D5, mid keeps 0x40=D6, small becomes 0x80=D7 — and TEMP3's comment is `;D7=CLOSE, D6=MIDDLE, D5=FAR`. Big distance -> FAR -> full-rate x2. Our flight.ts:62-66 has near:1.0 / far:0.375 — inverted. Held.
corrections: Numbers and size are right; scope needs one note. enemy.ts:173-177 (`depth < NEAR_DEPTH -> 'near'`) maps depth to band CORRECTLY and must NOT be touched — the fix is a pure swap of the two values in the DISCHK record (near: 0.375, far: 1.0), nothing else. Also delete the now-false flight.ts:24 header claim 'Apparent agility rises when something is near' and the mirroring line in docs/red-baron-1980-source-findings.md:149-151, which is the upstream source of the error.

reasoning: RBARON.MAC:3186-3189 reads `10$: BIT GRMODE / BPL 20$ / LDA I,40 ;GROUND MODE (SLOW MOTION) / 20$: STA TEMP3`. GRMODE's D7 is the ground flag (RBARON.MAC:134, `GRMODE: .BLKB 1 ;GROUND MODE (D7=GROUND)`), BIT puts it in N, and BPL skips the override when it is clear — so in ground mode the entire distance computation above is discarded and TEMP3 is forced to the literal. Radix at :3188 is 16 (.RADIX 16 at :74; the decimal island is :6217-6280), so `40` = 0x40 = bit 6 = D6 = MIDDLE = the 1.25x/0.625 band per the DISCHK decode I did for FL-002. Our GROUND_CONTROL_BAND = 'far' (flight.ts:76) resolves to 0.375. Wrong band. Held.
corrections: One clarification the finding does not make, and it matters for sequencing: 'mid' is 0.625 in BOTH our current (inverted) table and the corrected one, so this fix ('far' -> 'mid') is INDEPENDENT of FL-002 and lands correctly whether or not FL-002 has been applied. It does not need to wait. Also: the ROM's ground band (0.625) is FASTER than its close-object band (0.375) — 'slow motion' is only relative to the empty-sky 1.0 default; keep that in the comment or the next reader will 're-fix' it back to the slowest band.

reasoning: Attacked each link of the chain independently; all three hold. (1) DISCHK fall-through is `TXA / ASL / TAX / LDA TEMP1 / ADC TEMP1` — the ASL's carry feeds the ADC, so it is an exact 16-bit x2, not an identity (RBARON.MAC:3474-3478). If 1.0 meant identity the author would have just RTS'd; he explicitly shifts. (2) MULTB4 (RBARON.MAC:6180-6184) is `ASL / ROL TEMP1 / ASL / ROL TEMP1 / RTS` = a 16-bit x4, and PFMOTN calls it on the X path ONLY: `LDX I,0 / LDA PLDELX / BPL 30$ / DEX / 30$: STX TEMP1 / JSR MULTB4 / JSR DISCHK` (:3190-3196), whereas the Y path is `50$: LDA PLDELY / LDX PLDELY+1 / STX TEMP1 / JSR DISCHK` (:3237-3240) with no MULTB4. (3) Band ratios verified in FL-002. So dUNIV4X = PLDELX x {8,5,3} and dI4YPOS = PLDELY x {2.0,1.25,0.75}. Arithmetic re-derived from scratch: full dive PLDELY = -128 (post-.4WORD), empty sky x2 = -256/frame; band PLYMAX-PLYMIN = 1536-32 = 1504; 1504/256 = 5.875 frames x 96 ms = 0.564 s. Ours at near(1.0): 1504/32 = 47 frames = 4.51 s = 8.0x. Ours at far(0.375): 1504/12 = 125.3 frames = 12.03 s = 21.3x. The claim's numbers are arithmetically correct and the cadence is right (POTSCL at RBARON.MAC:778 and PFMOTN at :785 are both in MAIN, i.e. the 96 ms calc frame). Held — but see corrections: the headline multiplier is a compound, not this finding's own.
corrections: TWO scope errors that will cause a wrong fix if taken literally. (a) The '8x-to-21x' headline is the COMPOUND of FL-001 + FL-002 + FL-004. FL-004's OWN residual, once FL-001 (table x4) and FL-002 (band swap) have landed, is exactly x2 on altitude — the missing DISCHK baseline — and nothing more. A dev who applies FL-001 and then also multiplies the altitude rate by 8 will overshoot by 4x. State the fix as: multiply the DISCHK record by 2 (near 0.75, mid 1.25, far 2.0), which makes the absolute gains ROM-exact and subsumes FL-002. (b) The x8 world-X factor is NOT applicable to our code today. Our step() has no UNIV4X — it accumulates a `heading` yaw angle (flight.ts:181). Multiplying that heading by 8 would just make the yaw 8x faster, which is emphatically not what the ROM does. The X half of FL-004 is CONTINGENT on FL-013 replacing yaw with a lateral UNIV4X pan, and must be sequenced after it. Size m is right only if the Y half ships alone; the X half is part of FL-013's l.

reasoning: Decoded POT.X (RBARON.MAC:5897-5926) byte by byte. `LDA PTRNGE / LSR / STA TEMP1 / LDA POTVAL / SEC / SBC TEMP1 / SEC / SBC PLDELX / TAX` gives X = signed diff = (POTVAL - PTRNGE/2) - PLDELX. `BPL 5$ / JSR COMP` makes A = |diff|; `CMP I,3 / BCC 17$` is the 2-count deadband (|diff| <= 2 leaves PLDELX alone) — which matches our TURN_HYSTERESIS. Then `TXA` (restores the SIGNED diff) / `LSR / LSR / LSR` gives the unsigned byte >>3 in 0..31; `CMP I,10` (hex, = 16) splits it: 0..15 are genuine positive steps; 16..31 came from a negative diff and `ORA I,0E0` maps them to 0xF0..0xFF = -16..-1. I verified this is an exact arithmetic floor: diff=-1 -> 0xFF>>3 = 31 -> 0xFF = -1 = floor(-1/8); diff=-9 -> 247>>3 = 30 -> 0xFE = -2 = floor(-9/8); diff=-128 -> 128>>3 = 16 -> 0xF0 = -16. So step = floor(diff/8), a first-order lag of 12.5% of the residual error per calc frame. `TAY / BEQ 20$ ... LDA I,1` forces +1 when the shift yields 0 with a nonzero diff. Our easeTurnRate (flight.ts:158-162) is a constant 8-count ramp that then snaps the last <=8 counts — a different response SHAPE, and the auditor's two spot checks are right (|delta|=8: ROM steps 1, we step 8; |delta|=100: ROM steps 12, we step 8). Cadence confirmed: POTSCL/POT.X is called from MAIN at RBARON.MAC:778, i.e. the 96 ms calc frame, not the 250 Hz NMI. Held.
corrections: Two small precisions for the implementer. (1) The step is bounded [-16, +15], not symmetric — a full-scale positive diff of +127 yields +15 while -128 yields -16. Implement as `Math.floor(delta / 8)`, which reproduces both ends exactly; do NOT use a truncating divide (`(delta/8)|0`), which rounds negatives toward zero and would break the -1 floor. (2) The 'else step = 1' branch at 20$ only ever fires for POSITIVE diff in [3..7]; negatives never reach it because the arithmetic shift already floors them to -1. So the guard is `if (step === 0 && delta > 0) step = 1` — an unconditional `sign(delta)` there would be wrong (it can never fire for negatives, and writing it as if it could hides the asymmetry). Size s is correct: this is a one-function change.

reasoning: The verifiable half is true and the decisive half is unverifiable, and the finding says so itself. Verified: POT.X's target IS the raw centred pot count (RBARON.MAC:5897-5902), there IS no 40-count cap (I grepped every PLDELX writer — :1111 and :1239 are clears, :5921 is POT.X; nothing clamps it), PTRNGE IS auto-calibrated by the NMI (RBGRND.MAC:124-127 grows it, :140-142 decays it), the bank DOES saturate at |PLDELX|=32 (PFROTN = PLDELX x8, clamped to 0x100 at RBARON.MAC:3225-3236), and the evade check DOES demand |PLDELX| >= 0x1C = 28 (RBARON.MAC:1084-1087). But the entire ACTIONABLE claim — that 40 is too SMALL, that the bank should pin at 'roughly a third of stick', that full stick should keep building pan '~3x beyond' — rests solely on an unsourced guess that PTRNGE is '200+'. Nothing in the shipped modules pins PTRNGE: it is a runtime measurement of a physical POKEY pot's observed swing, and the only constraints the source offers are PTRNGE/2 <= 127 (PLDELX is a signed byte, `.BLKB 1` at :164) and PTRNGE > 0x10 (POTSCL's `CPY I,10 ;WAIT FOR SOME POT RANGE`). LETCHK (:1985-1998) uses PTRNGE only as a fraction of itself. If PTRNGE were ~80, MAX_TURN = 40 would be exactly right and there would be no finding at all. The finding is self-refuting on its own decisive number ('We cannot pin PTRNGE numerically from the source... so the exact full-scale is not citable') — you cannot show 40 is wrong without the number you concede you do not have. Per the brief, a claim resting on unknowable contents is REFUTED as unverifiable. Two further kills. (a) Its closing sentence, 'Ours does neither', is FALSE about our own code: with MAX_TURN=40 our bank saturates at 32/40 = 80% of stick (so it DOES saturate before full deflection) and the pan rate DOES keep climbing 1.25x beyond that point. Ours does both — less strongly, but the shape is not absent. (b) The proposed fix is not a fix: there is no POKEY pot and no runtime calibration in a browser, so 'feed the pot in raw counts (0..PTRNGE)' means replacing one invented constant (40) with a different invented constant (PTRNGE). That is size-m churn through the input plumbing for zero fidelity gain. Finally, MAX_TURN is not a hidden error — flight.ts:28-33 already declares it a deliberate tuning choice, and it satisfies both source-pinned invariants (>= 28 is reachable; x8 saturates 0x100).
corrections: If anything survives here it is a DOC change, not a code change, and it is size xs, not m: note in flight.ts that the ROM's X axis is NOT range-normalised (POTSCL at :5838-5846 divides the Y pot by PTRNGE+1 to get a fraction of full scale; POT.X feeds X as raw centred counts), so MAX_TURN is our stand-in for the unknowable PTRNGE/2 and is bounded above by 127. Do not re-plumb the input for this.

reasoning: I read the projection path end to end and there is genuinely no yaw. POSITP (RBGRND.MAC:261-268) loads MATH+4/+5 = UNIV4X (';X ABOUT CENTER') and MATH+6/+7 = 0 (';Y ABOUT PLAYER POSITION') — the rotation CENTRE. POSITH (:269-322) loads MATH+8/+9 = OBJECT.X and MATH+0A/+0B = OBJECT.Y - I4YPOS (';-EYE POSITION'), fires MATSTT twice, then perspective-divides: first the rotated Y by OBJECT+6/+7 (adding HORIZN) and then the rotated X by OBJECT+4/+5. The ONE angle the Math Box has been given is PFTRIG's, and PFTRIG (RBARON.MAC:5981-5983) is literally `LDA PFROTN+1 / LDY PFROTN` falling into TRIG — PFROTN is `;HORIZON ROTATION` (:166), the ROLL, computed each frame as PLDELX x8 clamped to 0x100 (:3214-3236). That rotation is in the world X-Y plane, i.e. a bank; depth (OBJECT+4/+5) is never rotated, never recomputed from any heading, and never touched by UNIV4X. So screen_x = [cos*(X - UNIV4X) - sin*(Y - I4YPOS)] / depth: turning increments UNIV4X (RBARON.MAC:3200-3206) and PFXSCR (:3207-3213), and objects are drawn at X - UNIV4X (corroborated at :2910, `SBC ZX,UNIV4X ;- UNIVERSE CENTER`). d(screen_x)/d(UNIV4X) scales as 1/depth — near objects sweep fast, far ones barely move. The player genuinely has no third axis and no heading: UNIV4X is `.BLKB 4` with I4YPOS = UNIV4X+2 (:90-91), so player state is (X, Y) and nothing else. Our camera.ts:45 composes a real `rotationY(attitude.yaw)` and flight.ts:197 feeds it `heading * YAW_SCALE`, which sweeps every depth at the same angular rate. Different flight model, not a different unit. Governing code confirmed live: main.ts:490 calls draw(toAttitude(flight), ...). Held, and size l is fair.
corrections: Two things the finding misses that change the work. (1) `toEye` (flight.ts:205) has NO consumers anywhere in src/ — I grepped; only toAttitude is wired (main.ts:490). So there is currently nowhere for a lateral pan to land: the fix is not merely 'drop rotationY', it must also thread an eye position (or a world-X offset) through flightView into the render path, which is why this is l and not m. (2) biplane.ts:145 REUSES toAttitude to derive the enemy's roll (`toAttitude({turnRate, pitchRate: 0, altitude: 0, heading: 0}).roll`). Removing `yaw` from the Attitude interface will break that call site and camera.ts's LEVEL constant; the enemy-roll reuse must survive the refactor. Also note the ROM does still pan the horizon laterally via PFXSCR (:3207-3213) — a distant horizon translating sideways reads very much like a slow yaw, so the visible difference is confined to NEAR geometry. That is the correct expectation to set, not 'the turn will look completely different'.
